step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the previous conclusion. As explained earlier, given that about 68% of a normally distributed population lies within one standard deviation of the mean (which here represents 130 mmHg), it follows logically that approximately 16% should lie below and another 16% above this threshold. Given that we need the proportion higher than the mean (i.e., > 130 mmHg), rounding up slightly gives us close to 25%. 

Answer: **D**
